steps.title: Identify the boundary line
steps.content: Examine the graph to locate the boundary between the shaded and unshaded regions. There is a horizontal line running across the graph where the color changes. This boundary is at $y = 36$.

steps.title: Determine the direction of the inequality
steps.content: The shaded (gray) region occupies all points above the horizontal line at $y = 36$. This means we are looking for all points where the $y$-coordinate is greater than 36. Since the boundary line itself is shaded (solid), the boundary is included in the solution set.

steps.title: Write the inequality
steps.content: The shaded region represents all points where $y$ is greater than or equal to 36. That matches choice D — D is correct.
